title: centaurea graeca subsp. ceccariniana
intro: Centaurea graeca subsp. ceccariniana, also known as Ceccarini's Knapweed, is a plant species in the daisy family with medicinal and ornamental value. It contains sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds that provide medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Centaurea graeca subsp. ceccariniana include potential uses in treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of tinctures, teas, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
